    )
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading HellaSwag: {e}")
    datasets_info["hellaswag"] = {"loaded": False}

# Train models - BASELINE (with dropout=0.2)
print("\n" + "=" * 60)
print("TRAINING BASELINE MODELS (Dropout=0.2)")
print("=" * 60)

baseline_models = {}
baseline_best_epochs = {}

for dataset_name in ["squad", "boolq", "hellaswag"]:
    if datasets_info[dataset_name]["loaded"]:
        print(f"\n### Training Baseline on {dataset_name.upper()} ###")
        torch.manual_seed(42)
        model = AdaptiveModalityModel(dropout_rate=0.2).to(device)
        model, best_epoch = train_model(
            model,
            datasets_info[dataset_name]["train_loader"],
            datasets_info[dataset_name]["val_loader"],
            dataset_name,
            "baseline",
            num_epochs=50,
            patience=10,
        )
        baseline_models[dataset_name] = model
        baseline_best_epochs[dataset_name] = best_epoch

# Train models - ABLATION (no dropout, dropout=0.0)
print("\n" + "=" * 60)
print("TRAINING ABLATION MODELS (No Dropout, Dropout=0.0)")
print("=" * 60)

ablation_models = {}
ablation_best_epochs = {}

for dataset_name in ["squad", "boolq", "hellaswag"]:
    if datasets_info[dataset_name]["loaded"]:
        print(f"\n### Training No-Dropout on {dataset_name.upper()} ###")
        torch.manual_seed(42)
        model = AdaptiveModalityModel(dropout_rate=0.0).to(device)
        model, best_epoch = train_model(
            model,
            datasets_info[dataset_name]["train_loader"],
            datasets_info[dataset_name]["val_loader"],
            dataset_name,
            "no_dropout",
            num_epochs=50,
            patience=10,
        )
        ablation_models[dataset_name] = model
        ablation_best_epochs[dataset_name] = best_epoch

# Visualizations
datasets = [d for d in ["squad", "boolq", "hellaswag"] if datasets_info[d]["loaded"]]

if len(datasets) > 0:
    fig = plt.figure(figsize=(20, 16))

    # Plot 1: TCE Comparison across datasets
    ax1 = plt.subplot(4, 3, 1)
    for i, dataset in enumerate(datasets):
        ax1.plot(
            experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["epochs"],
            experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["tce"]["val"],
            label=f"{dataset.upper()} (Baseline)",
            linestyle="-",
            linewidth=2,
        )
        ax1.plot(
            experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["epochs"],
            experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["tce"]["val"],
            label=f"{dataset.upper()} (No Dropout)",
            linestyle="--",
            linewidth=2,
        )
    ax1.set_xlabel("Epoch", fontsize=11)
    ax1.set_ylabel("Trust Calibration Error", fontsize=11)
    ax1.set_title(
        "TCE Comparison: Baseline vs No Dropout", fontsize=13, fontweight="bold"
    )
    ax1.legend(fontsize=8)
    ax1.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    # Plot 2: ARR Comparison across datasets
    ax2 = plt.subplot(4, 3, 2)
    for i, dataset in enumerate(datasets):
        ax2.plot(
            experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["epochs"],
            experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["arr"]["val"],
            label=f"{dataset.upper()} (Baseline)",
            linestyle="-",
            linewidth=2,
        )
        ax2.plot(
            experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["epochs"],
            experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["arr"]["val"],
            label=f"{dataset.upper()} (No Dropout)",
            linestyle="--",
            linewidth=2,
        )
    ax2.set_xlabel("Epoch", fontsize=11)
    ax2.set_ylabel("Appropriate Reliance Rate", fontsize=11)
    ax2.set_title(
        "ARR Comparison: Baseline vs No Dropout", fontsize=13, fontweight="bold"
    )
    ax2.legend(fontsize=8)
    ax2.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    # Plot 3: TCS Comparison across datasets
    ax3 = plt.subplot(4, 3, 3)
    for i, dataset in enumerate(datasets):
        ax3.plot(
            experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["epochs"],
            experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["tcs"]["val"],
            label=f"{dataset.upper()} (Baseline)",
            linestyle="-",
            linewidth=2,
        )
        ax3.plot(
            experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["epochs"],
            experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["tcs"]["val"],
            label=f"{dataset.upper()} (No Dropout)",
            linestyle="--",
            linewidth=2,
        )
    ax3.set_xlabel("Epoch", fontsize=11)
    ax3.set_ylabel("Trust Calibration Score", fontsize=11)
    ax3.set_title(
        "TCS Comparison: Baseline vs No Dropout", fontsize=13, fontweight="bold"
    )
    ax3.legend(fontsize=8)
    ax3.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    # Plots 4-6: Per-dataset TCE comparison
    for idx, dataset in enumerate(datasets):
        ax = plt.subplot(4, 3, 4 + idx)
        ax.plot(
            experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["epochs"],
            experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["tce"]["val"],
            label="Baseline (Dropout=0.2)",
            color="#E63946",
            linestyle="-",
            linewidth=2.5,
        )
        ax.plot(
            experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["epochs"],
            experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["tce"]["val"],
            label="No Dropout (Dropout=0.0)",
            color="#2A9D8F",
            linestyle="--",
            linewidth=2.5,
        )
        ax.set_xlabel("Epoch", fontsize=11)
        ax.set_ylabel("TCE", fontsize=11)
        ax.set_title(
            f"{dataset.upper()} - TCE Comparison", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold"
        )
        ax.legend(fontsize=9)
        ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    # Plots 7-9: Per-dataset loss comparison
    for idx, dataset in enumerate(datasets):
        ax = plt.subplot(4, 3, 7 + idx)
        ax.plot(
            experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["epochs"],
            experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["losses"]["val"],
            label="Baseline",
            color="#264653",
            linestyle="-",
            linewidth=2,
        )
        ax.plot(
            experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["epochs"],
            experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["losses"]["val"],
            label="No Dropout",
            color="#F4A261",
            linestyle="--",
            linewidth=2,
        )
        ax.set_xlabel("Epoch", fontsize=11)
        ax.set_ylabel("Validation Loss", fontsize=11)
        ax.set_title(

        )
        ax.legend(fontsize=9)
        ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    # Plot 10: Final metrics comparison bar chart
    ax10 = plt.subplot(4, 3, 10)
    metrics_names = ["TCE", "ARR", "TCS"]
    x = np.arange(len(datasets))
    width = 0.15

    for metric_idx, metric_name in enumerate(["tce", "arr", "tcs"]):
        baseline_vals = [
            experiment_data["baseline"][d][metric_name]["val"][-1] for d in datasets
        ]
        ablation_vals = [
            experiment_data["no_dropout"][d][metric_name]["val"][-1] for d in datasets
        ]

        ax10.bar(
            x + metric_idx * width * 2 - width,
            baseline_vals,
            width,
            label=f"{metrics_names[metric_idx]} (Baseline)",
            alpha=0.8,
        )
        ax10.bar(
            x + metric_idx * width * 2,
            ablation_vals,
            width,
            label=f"{metrics_names[metric_idx]} (No Dropout)",
            alpha=0.8,
        )

    ax10.set_xlabel("Dataset", fontsize=11)
    ax10.set_ylabel("Score", fontsize=11)
    ax10.set_title(
        "Final Validation Metrics Comparison", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold"
    )
    ax10.set_xticks(x + width * 1.5)
    ax10.set_xticklabels([d.upper() for d in datasets])
    ax10.legend(fontsize=8, ncol=2)
    ax10.grid(True, axis="y", alpha=0.3)

    # Plot 11: Relative improvement in TCE
    ax11 = plt.subplot(4, 3, 11)
    tce_improvements = []
    for dataset in datasets:
        baseline_tce = experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["tce"]["val"][-1]
        ablation_tce = experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["tce"]["val"][-1]
        improvement = ((baseline_tce - ablation_tce) / baseline_tce) * 100
        tce_improvements.append(improvement)

    colors_improvement = ["#2A9D8F" if x > 0 else "#E63946" for x in tce_improvements]
    ax11.bar(datasets, tce_improvements, color=colors_improvement, alpha=0.8)
    ax11.axhline(y=0, color="black", linestyle="-", linewidth=0.8)
    ax11.set_xlabel("Dataset", fontsize=11)
    ax11.set_ylabel("TCE Improvement (%)", fontsize=11)
    ax11.set_title(
        "TCE Improvement: Baseline → No Dropout", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold"
    )
    ax11.grid(True, axis="y", alpha=0.3)

    # Plot 12: Combined metric comparison
    ax12 = plt.subplot(4, 3, 12)
    baseline_combined = [
        experiment_data["baseline"][d]["arr"]["val"][-1]
        + experiment_data["baseline"][d]["tcs"]["val"][-1]
        - experiment_data["baseline"][d]["tce"]["val"][-1]
        for d in datasets
    ]
    ablation_combined = [
        experiment_data["no_dropout"][d]["arr"]["val"][-1]
        + experiment_data["no_dropout"][d]["tcs"]["val"][-1]
        - experiment_data["no_dropout"][d]["tce"]["val"][-1]
        for d in datasets
    ]

    x = np.arange(len(datasets))
    width = 0.35
    ax12.bar(
        x - width / 2,
        baseline_combined,
        width,
        label="Baseline",
        alpha=0.8,
        color="#264653",
    )
    ax12.bar(
        x + width / 2,
        ablation_combined,
        width,
        label="No Dropout",
        alpha=0.8,
        color="#E76F51",
    )
    ax12.set_xlabel("Dataset", fontsize=11)
    ax12.set_ylabel("Combined Metric (ARR+TCS-TCE)", fontsize=11)
    ax12.set_title("Combined Performance Metric", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")
    ax12.set_xticks(x)
    ax12.set_xticklabels([d.upper() for d in datasets])
    ax12.legend(fontsize=9)
    ax12.grid(True, axis="y", alpha=0.3)

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "ablation_no_dropout_results.png"),
        dpi=300,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    print(
        f"\nPlot saved to {os.path.join(working_dir, 'ablation_no_dropout_results.png')}"
    )

# Save experiment data
np.save(os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), experiment_data)
print(f"Experiment data saved to {os.path.join(working_dir, 'experiment_data.npy')}")

# Final summary
print("\n" + "=" * 80)
print("ABLATION STUDY RESULTS: REMOVE DROPOUT REGULARIZATION")
print("=" * 80)

for dataset in datasets:
    print(f"\n{'=' * 80}")
    print(f"{dataset.upper()} DATASET")
    print(f"{'=' * 80}")

    print("\nBASELINE (Dropout=0.2):")
    print(
        f"  Final Val TCE: {experiment_data['baseline'][dataset]['tce']['val'][-1]:.4f}"
    )
    print(
        f"  Final Val ARR: {experiment_data['baseline'][dataset]['arr']['val'][-1]:.4f}"
    )
    print(
        f"  Final Val TCS: {experiment_data['baseline'][dataset]['tcs']['val'][-1]:.4f}"
    )
    print(f"  Best Epoch: {baseline_best_epochs[dataset]}")

    print("\nABLATION (No Dropout, Dropout=0.0):")
    print(
        f"  Final Val TCE: {experiment_data['no_dropout'][dataset]['tce']['val'][-1]:.4f}"
    )
    print(
        f"  Final Val ARR: {experiment_data['no_dropout'][dataset]['arr']['val'][-1]:.4f}"
    )
    print(
        f"  Final Val TCS: {experiment_data['no_dropout'][dataset]['tcs']['val'][-1]:.4f}"
    )
    print(f"  Best Epoch: {ablation_best_epochs[dataset]}")

    print("\nCHANGE (Baseline → No Dropout):")
    tce_change = (
        experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["tce"]["val"][-1]
        - experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["tce"]["val"][-1]
    )
    arr_change = (
        experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["arr"]["val"][-1]
        - experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["arr"]["val"][-1]
    )
    tcs_change = (
        experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["tcs"]["val"][-1]
        - experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["tcs"]["val"][-1]
    )

    print(
        f"  ΔTCE: {tce_change:+.4f} {'(Lower is better)' if tce_change < 0 else '(Higher is worse)'}"
    )
    print(f"  ΔARR: {arr_change:+.4f} {'✓' if arr_change > 0 else '✗'}")
    print(f"  ΔTCS: {tcs_change:+.4f} {'✓' if tcs_change > 0 else '✗'}")

print("\n" + "=" * 80)
print("AVERAGE RESULTS ACROSS ALL DATASETS")
print("=" * 80)

baseline_avg_tce = np.mean(
    [experiment_data["baseline"][d]["tce"]["val"][-1] for d in datasets]
)
baseline_avg_arr = np.mean(
    [experiment_data["baseline"][d]["arr"]["val"][-1] for d in datasets]
)
baseline_avg_tcs = np.mean(
    [experiment_data["baseline"][d]["tcs"]["val"][-1] for d in datasets]
)

ablation_avg_tce = np.mean(
    [experiment_data["no_dropout"][d]["tce"]["val"][-1] for d in datasets]
)
ablation_avg_arr = np.mean(
    [experiment_data["no_dropout"][d]["arr"]["val"][-1] for d in datasets]
)
ablation_avg_tcs = np.mean(
    [experiment_data["no_dropout"][d]["tcs"]["val"][-1] for d in datasets]
)

print("\nBaseline (Dropout=0.2):")
print(f"  Avg TCE: {baseline_avg_tce:.4f}")
print(f"  Avg ARR: {baseline_avg_arr:.4f}")
print(f"  Avg TCS: {baseline_avg_tcs:.4f}")

print("\nAblation (No Dropout):")
print(f"  Avg TCE: {ablation_avg_tce:.4f}")
print(f"  Avg ARR: {ablation_avg_arr:.4f}")
print(f"  Avg TCS: {ablation_avg_tcs:.4f}")

print("\nAverage Change:")
print(f"  ΔTCE: {ablation_avg_tce - baseline_avg_tce:+.4f}")
print(f"  ΔARR: {ablation_avg_arr - baseline_avg_arr:+.4f}")
print(f"  ΔTCS: {ablation_avg_tcs - baseline_avg_tcs:+.4f}")

print("\n" + "=" * 80)
print("CONCLUSION")
print("=" * 80)

if ablation_avg_tce < baseline_avg_tce:
    print("✓ Removing dropout IMPROVED Trust Calibration Error (TCE).")
else:
    print("✗ Removing dropout WORSENED Trust Calibration Error (TCE).")

if ablation_avg_arr > baseline_avg_arr:
    print("✓ Removing dropout IMPROVED Appropriate Reliance Rate (ARR).")
else:
    print("✗ Removing dropout WORSENED Appropriate Reliance Rate (ARR).")

if ablation_avg_tcs > baseline_avg_tcs:
    print("✓ Removing dropout IMPROVED Trust Calibration Score (TCS).")
else:
    print("✗ Removing dropout WORSENED Trust Calibration Score (TCS).")

print("=" * 80)

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")

# Load experiment data
try:
    experiment_data = np.load(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), allow_pickle=True
    ).item()
    datasets = ["squad", "boolq", "hellaswag"]
    print("Successfully loaded experiment data")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading experiment data: {e}")
    experiment_data = None

if experiment_data:
    # Plot 1: TCE comparison across all datasets
    try:
        plt.figure(figsize=(14, 5))
        for i, dataset in enumerate(datasets):
            plt.subplot(1, 3, i + 1)
            plt.plot(
                experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["epochs"],
                experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["tce"]["val"],
                label="Baseline (Dropout=0.2)",
                linewidth=2,
                color="#E63946",
            )
            plt.plot(
                experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["epochs"],
                experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["tce"]["val"],
                label="No Dropout",
                linewidth=2,
                linestyle="--",
                color="#2A9D8F",
            )
            plt.xlabel("Epoch")
            plt.ylabel("Trust Calibration Error")
            plt.title(f"{dataset.upper()} Dataset - TCE Comparison")
            plt.legend()
            plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "tce_comparison_all_datasets.png"),
            dpi=300,
            bbox_inches="tight",
        )
        plt.close()
        print("Saved TCE comparison plot")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating TCE comparison plot: {e}")
        plt.close()

    # Plot 2: ARR comparison across all datasets
    try:
        plt.figure(figsize=(14, 5))
        for i, dataset in enumerate(datasets):
            plt.subplot(1, 3, i + 1)
            plt.plot(
                experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["epochs"],
                experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["arr"]["val"],
                label="Baseline (Dropout=0.2)",
                linewidth=2,
                color="#264653",
            )
            plt.plot(
                experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["epochs"],
                experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["arr"]["val"],
                label="No Dropout",
                linewidth=2,
                linestyle="--",
                color="#F4A261",
            )
            plt.xlabel("Epoch")
            plt.ylabel("Appropriate Reliance Rate")
            plt.title(f"{dataset.upper()} Dataset - ARR Comparison")
            plt.legend()
            plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "arr_comparison_all_datasets.png"),
            dpi=300,
            bbox_inches="tight",
        )
        plt.close()
        print("Saved ARR comparison plot")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating ARR comparison plot: {e}")
        plt.close()

    # Plot 3: TCS comparison across all datasets
    try:
        plt.figure(figsize=(14, 5))
        for i, dataset in enumerate(datasets):
            plt.subplot(1, 3, i + 1)
            plt.plot(
                experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["epochs"],
                experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["tcs"]["val"],
                label="Baseline (Dropout=0.2)",
                linewidth=2,
                color="#8338EC",
            )
            plt.plot(
                experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["epochs"],
                experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["tcs"]["val"],
                label="No Dropout",
                linewidth=2,
                linestyle="--",
                color="#3A86FF",
            )
            plt.xlabel("Epoch")
            plt.ylabel("Trust Calibration Score")
            plt.title(f"{dataset.upper()} Dataset - TCS Comparison")
            plt.legend()
            plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "tcs_comparison_all_datasets.png"),
            dpi=300,
            bbox_inches="tight",
        )
        plt.close()
        print("Saved TCS comparison plot")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating TCS comparison plot: {e}")
        plt.close()

    # Plot 4: Validation loss comparison
    try:
        plt.figure(figsize=(14, 5))
        for i, dataset in enumerate(datasets):
            plt.subplot(1, 3, i + 1)
            plt.plot(
                experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["epochs"],
                experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["losses"]["val"],
                label="Baseline (Dropout=0.2)",
                linewidth=2,
                color="#06FFA5",
            )
            plt.plot(
                experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["epochs"],
                experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset]["losses"]["val"],
                label="No Dropout",
                linewidth=2,
                linestyle="--",
                color="#FF006E",
            )
            plt.xlabel("Epoch")
            plt.ylabel("Validation Loss")
            plt.title(f"{dataset.upper()} Dataset - Loss Comparison")
            plt.legend()
            plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "loss_comparison_all_datasets.png"),
            dpi=300,
            bbox_inches="tight",
        )
        plt.close()
        print("Saved loss comparison plot")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating loss comparison plot: {e}")
        plt.close()

    # Plot 5: Final metrics bar chart
    try:
        fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(16, 5))
        metrics = ["tce", "arr", "tcs"]
        metric_names = [
            "TCE (Lower Better)",
            "ARR (Higher Better)",
            "TCS (Higher Better)",
        ]

        for idx, (metric, name) in enumerate(zip(metrics, metric_names)):
            baseline_vals = [
                experiment_data["baseline"][d][metric]["val"][-1] for d in datasets
            ]
            nodrop_vals = [
                experiment_data["no_dropout"][d][metric]["val"][-1] for d in datasets
            ]

            x = np.arange(len(datasets))
            width = 0.35
            axes[idx].bar(
                x - width / 2,
                baseline_vals,
                width,
                label="Baseline",
                alpha=0.8,
                color="#457B9D",
            )
            axes[idx].bar(
                x + width / 2,
                nodrop_vals,
                width,
                label="No Dropout",
                alpha=0.8,
                color="#E63946",
            )
            axes[idx].set_xlabel("Dataset")
            axes[idx].set_ylabel(name.split()[0])
            axes[idx].set_title(f"Final {name}")
            axes[idx].set_xticks(x)
            axes[idx].set_xticklabels([d.upper() for d in datasets])
            axes[idx].legend()
            axes[idx].grid(True, axis="y", alpha=0.3)

        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "final_metrics_comparison_all_datasets.png"),
            dpi=300,
            bbox_inches="tight",
        )
        plt.close()
        print("Saved final metrics comparison plot")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating final metrics bar chart: {e}")
        plt.close()

    # Plot 6: Metric improvements (percentage change)
    try:
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
        metrics = ["tce", "arr", "tcs"]
        metric_labels = ["TCE", "ARR", "TCS"]
        x = np.arange(len(datasets))
        width = 0.25

        for i, (metric, label) in enumerate(zip(metrics, metric_labels)):
            improvements = []
            for dataset in datasets:
                baseline_val = experiment_data["baseline"][dataset][metric]["val"][-1]
                nodrop_val = experiment_data["no_dropout"][dataset][metric]["val"][-1]
                if metric == "tce":
                    improvement = ((baseline_val - nodrop_val) / baseline_val) * 100
                else:
                    improvement = ((nodrop_val - baseline_val) / baseline_val) * 100
                improvements.append(improvement)

            ax.bar(x + i * width, improvements, width, label=label, alpha=0.8)

        ax.axhline(y=0, color="black", linestyle="-", linewidth=0.8)
        ax.set_xlabel("Dataset")
        ax.set_ylabel("Improvement (%)")
        ax.set_title("Ablation Study: Metric Improvements (Baseline → No Dropout)")
        ax.set_xticks(x + width)
        ax.set_xticklabels([d.upper() for d in datasets])
        ax.legend()
        ax.grid(True, axis="y", alpha=0.3)
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "metric_improvements_no_dropout_ablation.png"),
            dpi=300,
            bbox_inches="tight",
        )
        plt.close()
        print("Saved metric improvements plot")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating metric improvements plot: {e}")
        plt.close()
plot_plan: This experiment implements an ablation study comparing two model configurations (baseline with dropout=0.2 vs. no dropout) across three datasets (SQuAD, BoolQ, HellaSwag). The models predict user trust decisions and optimal modality selection, tracking metrics including Trust Calibration Error (TCE), Appropriate Reliance Rate (ARR), and Trust Calibration Score (TCS). I'll create visualizations showing training/validation curves for all metrics across both ablation conditions and all datasets, comparing performance differences to evaluate the impact of removing dropout regularization. The plots will include per-dataset comparisons, cross-dataset summaries, metric improvements, and combined performance scores.

plot_analyses.analysis:
Across SQUAD/BOOLQ/HELLASWAG, removing dropout (0.2→0.0) yields consistently lower validation loss but noticeably worse trust calibration error (TCE), with only small degradations in ARR/TCS. 

TCE dynamics: All datasets show rapid TCE reduction early (≈epochs 0–6) to near-zero for both conditions, followed by a late-epoch deterioration where No-Dropout spikes more strongly. On SQUAD, baseline ends around ~0.10 while No-Dropout rises to ~0.20 (≈2× worse). On BOOLQ, baseline ends ~0.04 while No-Dropout ends ~0.095 (≈2.2× worse). On HELLASWAG, TCE stays ~0 throughout and is largely insensitive to dropout. This pattern suggests dropout is acting primarily as a regularizer for the uncertainty/trust head (or whatever produces the trust signal), preventing late-training overconfidence/miscalibration even when predictive loss continues to improve.

ARR/TCS dynamics: ARR and TCS ramp quickly from ~0.80–0.86 to ~1.0 by ~epochs 4–6 for all datasets, then remain saturated for most of training. Late-epoch declines appear mainly on BOOLQ (and slightly on SQUAD): No-Dropout shows a larger end-of-training dip (e.g., BOOLQ ARR/TCS dropping to ~0.96) than baseline (staying closer to ~0.985). HELLASWAG remains at 1.0 for both. Because ARR/TCS are near-ceiling, they are less sensitive and may be masking meaningful differences; TCE is the discriminative metric here.

Final metric bars / improvement plots: Final TCE (lower better) clearly worsens without dropout on SQUAD and BOOLQ, while ARR/TCS change only marginally (small negative deltas). The “TCE Improvement” bar is strongly negative for SQUAD and BOOLQ (i.e., degradation), consistent with the time-series. Combined metric (ARR+TCS−TCE) decreases for SQUAD and BOOLQ under No-Dropout, and is essentially unchanged for HELLASWAG.

Interpretation for component analysis: Dropout appears to be a critical component for maintaining calibration/trust alignment, especially on SQUAD and BOOLQ. The fact that loss improves while TCE worsens indicates a decoupling between predictive fit and uncertainty quality—exactly the kind of phenomenon your paper targets (“beyond calibration/communication”). It also suggests that optimizing for task loss alone can harm uncertainty communication reliability.

Cross-dataset sensitivity: HELLASWAG is robust to dropout removal (all metrics saturate), implying either the task is easier for the model/metric setup, the trust signal is less challenged, or the evaluation is not sensitive enough on this dataset. SQUAD and BOOLQ show clear sensitivity, with BOOLQ exhibiting the largest relative TCE degradation and the clearest ARR/TCS late drop.

Experimental concerns / next steps: (1) The late-epoch TCE spike suggests overfitting of the calibration/trust module; consider early stopping on TCE rather than loss, or adding explicit calibration regularizers. (2) Because ARR/TCS saturate at 1.0 for long stretches, report more granular/continuous trust metrics or evaluate on harder splits to avoid ceiling effects. (3) Run multiple seeds and report std; the late-epoch divergence could be seed-sensitive. (4) If dropout is intended as part of “uncertainty estimation,” test whether MC-dropout at inference vs. dropout only during training changes TCE/ARR/TCS differently (this would connect directly to uncertainty communication mechanisms).

vlm_feedback_summary: Plots compare Baseline (dropout=0.2) vs No-Dropout across SQUAD/BOOLQ/HELLASWAG for TCE, ARR, TCS, and validation loss, plus final-metric bar charts and a combined metric. Main visible effect: No-Dropout reduces loss slightly but degrades TCE notably on SQUAD/BOOLQ; ARR/TCS largely saturate with small late drops (bigger for No-Dropout), while HELLASWAG is largely unaffected.

code:
import os
import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.optim as optim
from torch.utils.data import Dataset, DataLoader
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from datasets import load_dataset

# Setup working directory
working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")
os.makedirs(working_dir, exist_ok=True)

# Device setup
device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
print(f"Using device: {device}")

# Set random seeds
np.random.seed(42)
torch.manual_seed(42)

# Initialize experiment data storage for both baseline and ablation
experiment_data = {
    "baseline": {
        "squad": {
            "metrics": {},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "arr": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tcs": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tce": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "epochs": [],
            "stopped_at": 0,
        },
        "boolq": {
            "metrics": {},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "arr": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tcs": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tce": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "epochs": [],
            "stopped_at": 0,
        },
        "hellaswag": {
            "metrics": {},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "arr": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tcs": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tce": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "epochs": [],
            "stopped_at": 0,
        },
    },
    "ablation_fixed_epochs": {
        "squad": {
            "metrics": {},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "arr": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tcs": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tce": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "epochs": [],
        },
        "boolq": {
            "metrics": {},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "arr": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tcs": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tce": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "epochs": [],
        },
        "hellaswag": {
            "metrics": {},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "arr": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tcs": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tce": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "epochs": [],
        },
    },
}


# Generate synthetic data with modality effectiveness
def generate_modality_aware_data(dataset_name, hf_dataset, n_samples=800):
    np.random.seed(42)
    n_samples = min(n_samples, len(hf_dataset))
    indices = np.random.choice(len(hf_dataset), n_samples, replace=False)
    features_list, user_decisions_list, llm_correct_list, optimal_modality_list = (
        [],
        [],
        [],
        [],
    )

    for idx in indices:
        llm_certainty = np.random.beta(3, 2)
        task_difficulty = np.random.beta(2, 3)
        user_expertise = np.random.beta(2, 2)
        llm_correct = int(np.random.random() < (0.6 + 0.3 * llm_certainty))

        if user_expertise < 0.4 and task_difficulty < 0.5:
            optimal_modality = 0
            modality_effectiveness = 0.7 + 0.2 * llm_certainty
        elif user_expertise > 0.6 and llm_certainty > 0.6:
            optimal_modality = 1
            modality_effectiveness = 0.8 + 0.15 * llm_certainty
        else:
            optimal_modality = 2
            modality_effectiveness = 0.75 + 0.2 * (1 - task_difficulty)

        accept_prob = llm_certainty * modality_effectiveness
        if llm_correct:
            accept_prob = min(0.95, accept_prob * 1.2)
        else:
            accept_prob = max(0.05, accept_prob * 0.6)
        accept_prob += 0.15 * user_expertise * (1 if llm_correct else -1)
        accept_prob = np.clip(accept_prob, 0.05, 0.95)
        user_decision = int(np.random.random() < accept_prob)

        features_list.append(
            [llm_certainty, task_difficulty, user_expertise, llm_correct]
        )
        user_decisions_list.append(user_decision)
        llm_correct_list.append(llm_correct)
        optimal_modality_list.append(optimal_modality)

    return (
        np.array(features_list),
        np.array(user_decisions_list),
        np.array(llm_correct_list),
        np.array(optimal_modality_list),
    )


# Custom Dataset
class ModalityDataset(Dataset):
    def __init__(self, features, user_decisions, llm_correct, optimal_modality):
        self.features = torch.FloatTensor(features)
        self.user_decisions = torch.LongTensor(user_decisions)
        self.llm_correct = torch.LongTensor(llm_correct)
        self.optimal_modality = torch.LongTensor(optimal_modality)

    def __len__(self):
        return len(self.features)

    def __getitem__(self, idx):
        return (
            self.features[idx],
            self.user_decisions[idx],
            self.llm_correct[idx],
            self.optimal_modality[idx],
        )


# Adaptive Modality Selector Network
class AdaptiveModalityModel(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim=4, hidden_dim=64):
        super(AdaptiveModalityModel, self).__init__()
        self.modality_selector = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(input_dim, hidden_dim),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(0.2),
            nn.Linear(hidden_dim, 3),
        )
        self.decision_predictor = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(input_dim + 3, hidden_dim),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(0.2),
            nn.Linear(hidden_dim, 2),
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        modality_logits = self.modality_selector(x)
        modality_probs = torch.softmax(modality_logits, dim=1)
        combined = torch.cat([x, modality_probs], dim=1)
        decision_logits = self.decision_predictor(combined)
        return decision_logits, modality_logits


# Calculate metrics
def calculate_trust_calibration_error(
    predicted_decisions, llm_correct, llm_certainty, n_bins=5
):
    predicted_decisions = (
        predicted_decisions.cpu().numpy()
        if torch.is_tensor(predicted_decisions)
        else predicted_decisions
    )
    llm_correct = (
        llm_correct.cpu().numpy() if torch.is_tensor(llm_correct) else llm_correct
    )
    llm_certainty = (
        llm_certainty.cpu().numpy() if torch.is_tensor(llm_certainty) else llm_certainty
    )
    bins = np.linspace(0, 1, n_bins + 1)
    tce, valid_bins = 0.0, 0
    for i in range(n_bins):
        mask = (llm_certainty >= bins[i]) & (llm_certainty < bins[i + 1])
        if mask.sum() > 0:
            trust_rate = predicted_decisions[mask].mean()
            accuracy_rate = llm_correct[mask].mean()
            tce += abs(trust_rate - accuracy_rate)
            valid_bins += 1
    return tce / max(valid_bins, 1)


def calculate_appropriate_reliance(predicted_decisions, llm_correct):
    predicted_decisions = (
        predicted_decisions.cpu().numpy()
        if torch.is_tensor(predicted_decisions)
        else predicted_decisions
    )
    llm_correct = (
        llm_correct.cpu().numpy() if torch.is_tensor(llm_correct) else llm_correct
    )
    correct_acceptances = np.sum((predicted_decisions == 1) & (llm_correct == 1))
    correct_rejections = np.sum((predicted_decisions == 0) & (llm_correct == 0))
    return (correct_acceptances + correct_rejections) / len(predicted_decisions)


def calculate_trust_calibration(predicted_decisions, llm_correct):
    predicted_decisions = (
        predicted_decisions.cpu().numpy()
        if torch.is_tensor(predicted_decisions)
        else predicted_decisions
    )
    llm_correct = (
        llm_correct.cpu().numpy() if torch.is_tensor(llm_correct) else llm_correct
    )
    trust_rate = np.mean(predicted_decisions)
    accuracy_rate = np.mean(llm_correct)
    return 1.0 - abs(trust_rate - accuracy_rate)


# Training function with early stopping (baseline)
def train_model_with_early_stopping(
    model, train_loader, val_loader, dataset_name, num_epochs=50, patience=10
):
    criterion_decision = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
    criterion_modality = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
    optimizer = optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=0.0005)
    best_metric, patience_counter, best_epoch, best_model_state = (
        0.0,
        0,
        0,
        model.state_dict(),
    )

    for epoch in range(num_epochs):
        model.train()
        (
            train_loss,
            train_predictions,
            train_llm_correct_list,
            train_llm_certainty_list,
        ) = (0.0, [], [], [])

        for (
            features_batch,
            decisions_batch,
            llm_correct_batch,
            optimal_modality_batch,
        ) in train_loader:
            features_batch, decisions_batch = features_batch.to(
                device
            ), decisions_batch.to(device)
            llm_correct_batch, optimal_modality_batch = llm_correct_batch.to(
                device
            ), optimal_modality_batch.to(device)

            optimizer.zero_grad()
            decision_outputs, modality_outputs = model(features_batch)
            loss = criterion_decision(
                decision_outputs, decisions_batch
            ) + 0.3 * criterion_modality(modality_outputs, optimal_modality_batch)
            loss.backward()
            optimizer.step()

            train_loss += loss.item()
            _, predicted = torch.max(decision_outputs, 1)
            train_predictions.extend(predicted.cpu().numpy())
            train_llm_correct_list.extend(llm_correct_batch.cpu().numpy())
            train_llm_certainty_list.extend(features_batch[:, 0].cpu().numpy())

        train_loss /= len(train_loader)
        train_arr = calculate_appropriate_reliance(
            np.array(train_predictions), np.array(train_llm_correct_list)
        )
        train_tcs = calculate_trust_calibration(
            np.array(train_predictions), np.array(train_llm_correct_list)
        )
        train_tce = calculate_trust_calibration_error(
            np.array(train_predictions),
            np.array(train_llm_correct_list),
            np.array(train_llm_certainty_list),
        )

        model.eval()
        val_loss, val_predictions, val_llm_correct_list, val_llm_certainty_list = (
            0.0,
            [],
            [],
            [],
        )
        with torch.no_grad():
            for (
                features_batch,
                decisions_batch,
                llm_correct_batch,
                optimal_modality_batch,
            ) in val_loader:
                features_batch, decisions_batch = features_batch.to(
                    device
                ), decisions_batch.to(device)
                llm_correct_batch, optimal_modality_batch = llm_correct_batch.to(
                    device
                ), optimal_modality_batch.to(device)

                decision_outputs, modality_outputs = model(features_batch)
                loss = criterion_decision(
                    decision_outputs, decisions_batch
                ) + 0.3 * criterion_modality(modality_outputs, optimal_modality_batch)
                val_loss += loss.item()
                _, predicted = torch.max(decision_outputs, 1)
                val_predictions.extend(predicted.cpu().numpy())
                val_llm_correct_list.extend(llm_correct_batch.cpu().numpy())
                val_llm_certainty_list.extend(features_batch[:, 0].cpu().numpy())

        val_loss /= len(val_loader)
        val_arr = calculate_appropriate_reliance(
            np.array(val_predictions), np.array(val_llm_correct_list)
        )
        val_tcs = calculate_trust_calibration(
            np.array(val_predictions), np.array(val_llm_correct_list)
        )
        val_tce = calculate_trust_calibration_error(
            np.array(val_predictions),
            np.array(val_llm_correct_list),
            np.array(val_llm_certainty_list),
        )

        experiment_data["baseline"][dataset_name]["losses"]["train"].append(train_loss)
        experiment_data["baseline"][dataset_name]["losses"]["val"].append(val_loss)
        experiment_data["baseline"][dataset_name]["arr"]["train"].append(train_arr)
        experiment_data["baseline"][dataset_name]["arr"]["val"].append(val_arr)
        experiment_data["baseline"][dataset_name]["tcs"]["train"].append(train_tcs)
        experiment_data["baseline"][dataset_name]["tcs"]["val"].append(val_tcs)
        experiment_data["baseline"][dataset_name]["tce"]["train"].append(train_tce)
        experiment_data["baseline"][dataset_name]["tce"]["val"].append(val_tce)
        experiment_data["baseline"][dataset_name]["epochs"].append(epoch)

        combined_metric = val_arr + val_tcs - val_tce

        if epoch % 10 == 0:
            print(
                f"Epoch {epoch}: train_loss={train_loss:.4f}, val_loss={val_loss:.4f}, Val ARR={val_arr:.4f}, TCS={val_tcs:.4f}, TCE={val_tce:.4f}"
            )

        if combined_metric > best_metric:
            best_metric, best_epoch, patience_counter, best_model_state = (
                combined_metric,
                epoch,
                0,
                model.state_dict(),
            )
        else:
            patience_counter += 1
            if patience_counter >= patience:
                print(f"Early stopping at epoch {epoch}. Best epoch: {best_epoch}")
                experiment_data["baseline"][dataset_name]["stopped_at"] = epoch
                break

    if patience_counter < patience:
        experiment_data["baseline"][dataset_name]["stopped_at"] = num_epochs - 1

    model.load_state_dict(best_model_state)
    return model, best_epoch


# Training function without early stopping (ablation)
def train_model_fixed_epochs(
    model, train_loader, val_loader, dataset_name, num_epochs=50
):
    criterion_decision = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
    criterion_modality = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
    optimizer = optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=0.0005)

    for epoch in range(num_epochs):
        model.train()
        (
            train_loss,
            train_predictions,
            train_llm_correct_list,
            train_llm_certainty_list,
        ) = (0.0, [], [], [])

        for (
            features_batch,
            decisions_batch,
            llm_correct_batch,
            optimal_modality_batch,
        ) in train_loader:
            features_batch, decisions_batch = features_batch.to(
                device
            ), decisions_batch.to(device)
            llm_correct_batch, optimal_modality_batch = llm_correct_batch.to(
                device
            ), optimal_modality_batch.to(device)

            optimizer.zero_grad()
            decision_outputs, modality_outputs = model(features_batch)
            loss = criterion_decision(
                decision_outputs, decisions_batch
            ) + 0.3 * criterion_modality(modality_outputs, optimal_modality_batch)
            loss.backward()
            optimizer.step()

            train_loss += loss.item()
            _, predicted = torch.max(decision_outputs, 1)
            train_predictions.extend(predicted.cpu().numpy())
            train_llm_correct_list.extend(llm_correct_batch.cpu().numpy())
            train_llm_certainty_list.extend(features_batch[:, 0].cpu().numpy())

        train_loss /= len(train_loader)
        train_arr = calculate_appropriate_reliance(
            np.array(train_predictions), np.array(train_llm_correct_list)
        )
        train_tcs = calculate_trust_calibration(
            np.array(train_predictions), np.array(train_llm_correct_list)
        )
        train_tce = calculate_trust_calibration_error(
            np.array(train_predictions),
            np.array(train_llm_correct_list),
            np.array(train_llm_certainty_list),
        )

        model.eval()
        val_loss, val_predictions, val_llm_correct_list, val_llm_certainty_list = (
            0.0,
            [],
            [],
            [],
        )
        with torch.no_grad():
            for (
                features_batch,
                decisions_batch,
                llm_correct_batch,
                optimal_modality_batch,
            ) in val_loader:
                features_batch, decisions_batch = features_batch.to(
                    device
                ), decisions_batch.to(device)
                llm_correct_batch, optimal_modality_batch = llm_correct_batch.to(
                    device
                ), optimal_modality_batch.to(device)

                decision_outputs, modality_outputs = model(features_batch)
                loss = criterion_decision(
                    decision_outputs, decisions_batch
                ) + 0.3 * criterion_modality(modality_outputs, optimal_modality_batch)
                val_loss += loss.item()
                _, predicted = torch.max(decision_outputs, 1)
                val_predictions.extend(predicted.cpu().numpy())
                val_llm_correct_list.extend(llm_correct_batch.cpu().numpy())
                val_llm_certainty_list.extend(features_batch[:, 0].cpu().numpy())

        val_loss /= len(val_loader)
        val_arr = calculate_appropriate_reliance(
            np.array(val_predictions), np.array(val_llm_correct_list)
        )
        val_tcs = calculate_trust_calibration(
            np.array(val_predictions), np.array(val_llm_correct_list)
        )
        val_tce = calculate_trust_calibration_error(
            np.array(val_predictions),
            np.array(val_llm_correct_list),
            np.array(val_llm_certainty_list),
        )

        experiment_data["ablation_fixed_epochs"][dataset_name]["losses"][
            "train"
        ].append(train_loss)
        experiment_data["ablation_fixed_epochs"][dataset_name]["losses"]["val"].append(
            val_loss
        )
        experiment_data["ablation_fixed_epochs"][dataset_name]["arr"]["train"].append(
            train_arr
        )
        experiment_data["ablation_fixed_epochs"][dataset_name]["arr"]["val"].append(
            val_arr
        )
        experiment_data["ablation_fixed_epochs"][dataset_name]["tcs"]["train"].append(
            train_tcs
        )
        experiment_data["ablation_fixed_epochs"][dataset_name]["tcs"]["val"].append(
            val_tcs
        )
        experiment_data["ablation_fixed_epochs"][dataset_name]["tce"]["train"].append(
            train_tce
        )
        experiment_data["ablation_fixed_epochs"][dataset_name]["tce"]["val"].append(
            val_tce
        )
        experiment_data["ablation_fixed_epochs"][dataset_name]["epochs"].append(epoch)

        if epoch % 10 == 0:
            print(
                f"Epoch {epoch}: train_loss={train_loss:.4f}, val_loss={val_loss:.4f}, Val ARR={val_arr:.4f}, TCS={val_tcs:.4f}, TCE={val_tce:.4f}"
            )

    return model


# Load datasets
print("\n=== Loading Datasets ===\n")
datasets_info = {}

# SQuAD
print("### Processing SQuAD ###")
try:
    squad_dataset = load_dataset("squad", split="validation")
    squad_features, squad_decisions, squad_llm_correct, squad_optimal_modality = (
        generate_modality_aware_data("squad", squad_dataset, n_samples=800)
    )
    (
        squad_train_features,

        squad_train_llm_correct,

# BoolQ

# HellaSwag
print("\n### Processing HellaSwag ###")
try:
    hellaswag_dataset = load_dataset("hellaswag", split="validation")
    (

except Exception as e:

    datasets_info["hellaswag"] = {"loaded": False}

# Train models
print("\n=== Training Models ===\n")

datasets = [d for d in ["squad", "boolq", "hellaswag"] if datasets_info[d]["loaded"]]

for dataset in datasets:
    print(f"\n{'='*60}")

    # Baseline with early stopping
    print(f"\n### BASELINE (with Early Stopping) - {dataset.upper()} ###")

# Save experiment data
np.save(os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), experiment_data)

# Final summary

for dataset in datasets:

    )
    print(
        f"    Final Val ARR: {experiment_data['baseline'][dataset]['arr']['val'][-1]:.4f}"
    )
    print(
        f"    Final Val TCS: {experiment_data['baseline'][dataset]['tcs']['val'][-1]:.4f}"
    )
    print(
        f"    Final Val TCE: {experiment_data['baseline'][dataset]['tce']['val'][-1]:.4f}"
    )
    print(f"  ABLATION (Fixed 50 Epochs):")
    print(
        f"    Final Val ARR: {experiment_data['ablation_fixed_epochs'][dataset]['arr']['val'][-1]:.4f}"
    )
    print(
        f"    Final Val TCS: {experiment_data['ablation_fixed_epochs'][dataset]['tcs']['val'][-1]:.4f}"
    )
    print(
        f"    Final Val TCE: {experiment_data['ablation_fixed_epochs'][dataset]['tce']['val'][-1]:.4f}"
    )

    arr_diff = (
        experiment_data["ablation_fixed_epochs"][dataset]["arr"]["val"][-1]
        - experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["arr"]["val"][-1]
    )
    tcs_diff = (
        experiment_data["ablation_fixed_epochs"][dataset]["tcs"]["val"][-1]
        - experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["tcs"]["val"][-1]
    )
    tce_diff = (
        experiment_data["ablation_fixed_epochs"][dataset]["tce"]["val"][-1]
        - experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["tce"]["val"][-1]
    )
    print(f"  DIFFERENCE (Ablation - Baseline):")
    print(f"    ARR: {arr_diff:+.4f}")
    print(f"    TCS: {tcs_diff:+.4f}")
    print(f"    TCE: {tce_diff:+.4f} (negative is better)")

print("\n" + "=" * 80)
print("CONCLUSION:")
if len(datasets) > 0:
    avg_arr_baseline = np.mean(
        [experiment_data["baseline"][d]["arr"]["val"][-1] for d in datasets]
    )
    avg_arr_ablation = np.mean(
        [
            experiment_data["ablation_fixed_epochs"][d]["arr"]["val"][-1]
            for d in datasets
        ]
    )
    avg_tcs_baseline = np.mean(
        [experiment_data["baseline"][d]["tcs"]["val"][-1] for d in datasets]
    )
    avg_tcs_ablation = np.mean(
        [
            experiment_data["ablation_fixed_epochs"][d]["tcs"]["val"][-1]
            for d in datasets
        ]
    )
    avg_tce_baseline = np.mean(
        [experiment_data["baseline"][d]["tce"]["val"][-1] for d in datasets]
    )
    avg_tce_ablation = np.mean(
        [
            experiment_data["ablation_fixed_epochs"][d]["tce"]["val"][-1]
            for d in datasets
        ]
    )

    print(
        f"Average Baseline ARR: {avg_arr_baseline:.4f}, Average Ablation ARR: {avg_arr_ablation:.4f}"
    )
    print(
        f"Average Baseline TCS: {avg_tcs_baseline:.4f}, Average Ablation TCS: {avg_tcs_ablation:.4f}"
    )
    print(
        f"Average Baseline TCE: {avg_tce_baseline:.4f}, Average Ablation TCE: {avg_tce_ablation:.4f}"
    )

    if (
        avg_arr_ablation > avg_arr_baseline
        and avg_tcs_ablation > avg_tcs_baseline
        and avg_tce_ablation < avg_tce_baseline
    ):
        print(
            "\nEarly stopping appears to be HARMFUL - fixed epoch training performs better."
        )
    elif (
        avg_arr_baseline > avg_arr_ablation
        and avg_tcs_baseline > avg_tcs_ablation
        and avg_tce_baseline < avg_tce_ablation
    ):
        print(
            "\nEarly stopping appears to be BENEFICIAL - prevents overfitting and maintains better performance."
        )
    else:
        print(
            "\nResults are MIXED - early stopping benefits vary by dataset and metric."
        )

print("=" * 80)

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")

try:
    experiment_data = np.load(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), allow_pickle=True
    ).item()
    datasets = ["squad", "boolq", "hellaswag"]
    colors = {"baseline": "#2E86AB", "ablation": "#E63946"}

    # Plot 1: Loss curves for all datasets
    try:
        fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))
        for i, dataset in enumerate(datasets):
            axes[i].plot(
                experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["epochs"],
                experiment_data["baseline"][dataset]["losses"]["val"],
                label="Baseline (Early Stop)",
                color=colors["baseline"],
                linewidth=2,
            )
            axes[i].plot(
                experiment_data["ablation_fixed_epochs"][dataset]["epochs"],
                experiment_data["ablation_fixed_epochs"][dataset]["losses"]["val"],
                label="Ablation (Fixed)",
                color=colors["ablation"],
                linewidth=2,

plot_plan: I will create visualizations comparing the baseline model (with early stopping) against the ablation study (fixed 50 epochs) across three datasets (SQuAD, BoolQ, HellaSwag). The plots will show training curves for loss, ARR (Appropriate Reliance Rate), TCS (Trust Calibration Score), and TCE (Trust Calibration Error), along with comparison bar charts for final metrics. I'll mark where early stopping occurred on the baseline curves and compare the final performance to determine if early stopping is beneficial or harmful for this adaptive modality selection task.

plot_analyses.analysis: Validation loss curves (SQUAD/BOOLQ/HELLASWAG) show baseline (early stop) and ablation (fixed) essentially overlapping throughout training. The dashed early-stop marker is ~epoch 16–17 for SQUAD/BOOLQ and ~epoch 13 for HELLASWAG. Despite continued loss improvements out to 50 epochs, the early-stop choice indicates the selected baseline checkpoint is much earlier than the minimum-loss epoch, implying the selection criterion is not pure loss minimization but likely tied to trust/uncertainty metrics. Key implication: the ablated component does not materially affect predictive loss/fit, so downstream differences in ARR/TCS/TCE can be attributed to uncertainty/trust behavior rather than accuracy changes (good for isolating communication effects).

ARR trajectories (SQUAD/BOOLQ/HELLASWAG) rise quickly to ~1.0 in early epochs, then degrade steadily after the early-stop point. By epoch 50, ARR is notably lower: ~0.895 (SQUAD), ~0.84 (BOOLQ), ~0.85 (HELLASWAG). The early-stop epoch aligns with the onset of ARR decline, suggesting overtraining harms appropriate reliance even while loss continues to decrease—classic accuracy–calibration/trust trade-off. The baseline vs ablation lines appear coincident in these time-series panels, so differences are better read from the final comparison bars; nevertheless, the temporal pattern suggests that any component intended to preserve reliance should be evaluated at matched loss/accuracy checkpoints, not just final epoch.

Final metric bar charts: (i) ARR: baseline near ceiling (~0.99–1.0) across datasets; ablation drops to ~0.89 (SQUAD), ~0.84 (BOOLQ), ~0.86 (HELLASWAG), i.e., absolute decreases of ~0.10–0.15 (≈10–15%). (ii) TCS mirrors ARR: baseline ~0.99–1.0; ablation ~0.91 (SQUAD), ~0.85 (BOOLQ), ~0.86 (HELLASWAG), similar magnitude drops. (iii) TCE (lower better): baseline is low (~0.10 SQUAD, ~0.04 BOOLQ, ~0.00 HELLASWAG), while ablation is much higher (~0.31 SQUAD, ~0.25 BOOLQ, ~0.27 HELLASWAG). This is a large degradation in calibration error (roughly 3× on SQUAD, ~6× on BOOLQ, and from ~0 to substantial on HELLASWAG). (iv) Training epochs: baseline uses early stopping (~14–17 epochs), ablation trains full 50 epochs. This confounds component effect with training regime: the ablation condition is both missing a component and not early-stopped, which could itself worsen trust/calibration metrics. To claim component contribution, you need matched training/selection (e.g., apply early stopping to ablation using same criterion, or evaluate both at the same epoch/checkpoint).

Multi-metric per-epoch grid (loss/ARR/TCS/TCE): TCS and ARR curves are nearly identical in shape and values across datasets, implying they may be computed from closely related signals (or even the same underlying measure). Both peak early and then decline with further training. TCE shows the complementary pattern: it drops rapidly to ~0 by ~epoch 6–15, then increases sharply after ~epoch 16–20, reaching ~0.25–0.32 by epoch 50. The early-stop marker sits right before the TCE blow-up, reinforcing that early stopping is acting as a regularizer for trust calibration. Notably, HELLASWAG TCE stays near 0 until ~epoch 17 then jumps with high variance (spikes around epochs mid-20s), suggesting instability in uncertainty estimates or binning/sparsity effects; this dataset may be more sensitive to overfitting in the uncertainty head or to distribution shift between train/val.

Standalone TCS panels reiterate that trust calibration score declines after early stop, with BOOLQ and HELLASWAG ending around mid-0.8s and SQUAD around low-0.9s. The decline is steeper for BOOLQ/HELLASWAG than SQUAD, indicating dataset-specific sensitivity: yes/no and multi-choice commonsense tasks may be more prone to overconfident wrong answers post-overtraining, harming trust calibration more than extractive QA.

Standalone TCE panels confirm the post-early-stop increase in calibration error, with SQUAD reaching ~0.31, BOOLQ ~0.25, HELLASWAG ~0.28–0.30 by epoch 50. The early regime where TCE≈0 is suspiciously perfect; it may indicate metric saturation/degeneracy (e.g., predictions all in one confidence bin, or a thresholding artifact) rather than truly perfect calibration. If TCE is computed from trust signals rather than probabilistic outputs, verify the implementation: check bin counts, smoothing, and whether the metric is clipped at 0. Also ensure that TCE is computed on the same examples as ARR/TCS and that confidence values have sufficient resolution.

Final metric bar charts repeated: they consistently show large gaps between baseline and ablation on ARR/TCS/TCE and large gap in training epochs (baseline early-stopped, ablation fixed 50). The consistency across the duplicated summary figure strengthens the observation but not the causal attribution. The most actionable takeaway is that whatever is removed (or the lack of early stopping) dramatically harms trust-related metrics while leaving loss unchanged—supporting the paper’s thesis that uncertainty/trust communication mechanisms can change human-facing reliability without changing task loss. However, to make this a clean component ablation, you must disentangle ‘component removed’ from ‘training longer’ by controlling epochs/checkpoint selection.

vlm_feedback_summary: Plots show baseline vs ablation across SQUAD/BOOLQ/HELLASWAG. Predictive validation loss is essentially identical between conditions, but trust/uncertainty metrics diverge strongly in final summaries: ablation yields lower ARR/TCS and much higher TCE. Overtraining past an early-stop point improves loss while degrading ARR/TCS and increasing TCE, suggesting a trade-off and making early stopping/checkpoint selection critical. A major confound is that baseline uses early stopping (~14–17 epochs) while ablation trains 50 epochs; component contribution claims require matched training or identical checkpointing criteria.

overall_plan:
Ablation name: Remove Learning Rate Scheduler (Constant Learning Rate).
I'll implement an ablation study that adds a ReduceLROnPlateau scheduler to the baseline constant learning rate approach. The scheduler will monitor the combined validation metric (ARR+TCS-TCE) and reduce the learning rate when it plateaus, allowing for finer adjustments during later training stages. I'll track and compare both approaches across all three datasets (SQuAD, BoolQ, HellaSwag), storing learning rates over time and final performance metrics. The results will show whether adaptive learning rate adjustment improves trust calibration and appropriate reliance compared to the constant learning rate baseline.

import os
import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.optim as optim
from torch.utils.data import Dataset, DataLoader
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from datasets import load_dataset

# Setup working directory
working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")
os.makedirs(working_dir, exist_ok=True)

# Device setup
device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
print(f"Using device: {device}")

# Set random seeds
np.random.seed(42)
torch.manual_seed(42)

# Initialize experiment data storage
experiment_data = {
    "baseline_constant_lr": {
        "squad": {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "arr": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tcs": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tce": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "epochs": [],
            "lr": [],
        },
        "boolq": {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "arr": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tcs": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tce": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "epochs": [],
            "lr": [],
        },
        "hellaswag": {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "arr": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tcs": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tce": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "epochs": [],
            "lr": [],
        },
    },
    "ablation_with_scheduler": {
        "squad": {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "arr": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tcs": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tce": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "epochs": [],
            "lr": [],
        },
        "boolq": {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "arr": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tcs": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tce": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "epochs": [],
            "lr": [],
        },
        "hellaswag": {
            "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "arr": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tcs": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "tce": {"train": [], "val": []},
            "epochs": [],
            "lr": [],
        },
    },
}


# Generate synthetic data with modality effectiveness
def generate_modality_aware_data(dataset_name, hf_dataset, n_samples=800):
    """Generate features with modality-aware ground truth."""
    np.random.seed(42)
    n_samples = min(n_samples, len(hf_dataset))
    indices = np.random.choice(len(hf_dataset), n_samples, replace=False)

    features_list = []
    user_decisions_list = []
    llm_correct_list = []
    optimal_modality_list = []

    for idx in indices:
        llm_certainty = np.random.beta(3, 2)
        task_difficulty = np.random.beta(2, 3)
        user_expertise = np.random.beta(2, 2)
        llm_correct = int(np.random.random() < (0.6 + 0.3 * llm_certainty))

        if user_expertise < 0.4 and task_difficulty < 0.5:
            optimal_modality = 0
            modality_effectiveness = 0.7 + 0.2 * llm_certainty
        elif user_expertise > 0.6 and llm_certainty > 0.6:
            optimal_modality = 1
            modality_effectiveness = 0.8 + 0.15 * llm_certainty
        else:
            optimal_modality = 2
            modality_effectiveness = 0.75 + 0.2 * (1 - task_difficulty)

        accept_prob = llm_certainty * modality_effectiveness
        if llm_correct:
            accept_prob = min(0.95, accept_prob * 1.2)
        else:
            accept_prob = max(0.05, accept_prob * 0.6)

        accept_prob += 0.15 * user_expertise * (1 if llm_correct else -1)
        accept_prob = np.clip(accept_prob, 0.05, 0.95)
        user_decision = int(np.random.random() < accept_prob)

        features_list.append(
            [llm_certainty, task_difficulty, user_expertise, llm_correct]
        )
        user_decisions_list.append(user_decision)
        llm_correct_list.append(llm_correct)
        optimal_modality_list.append(optimal_modality)

    return (
        np.array(features_list),
        np.array(user_decisions_list),
        np.array(llm_correct_list),
        np.array(optimal_modality_list),
    )


# Custom Dataset
class ModalityDataset(Dataset):
    def __init__(self, features, user_decisions, llm_correct, optimal_modality):
        self.features = torch.FloatTensor(features)
        self.user_decisions = torch.LongTensor(user_decisions)
        self.llm_correct = torch.LongTensor(llm_correct)
        self.optimal_modality = torch.LongTensor(optimal_modality)

    def __len__(self):
        return len(self.features)

    def __getitem__(self, idx):
        return (
            self.features[idx],
            self.user_decisions[idx],
            self.llm_correct[idx],
            self.optimal_modality[idx],
        )


# Adaptive Modality Selector Network
class AdaptiveModalityModel(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim=4, hidden_dim=64):
        super(AdaptiveModalityModel, self).__init__()
        self.modality_selector = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(input_dim, hidden_dim),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(0.2),
            nn.Linear(hidden_dim, 3),
        )
        self.decision_predictor = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(input_dim + 3, hidden_dim),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(0.2),
            nn.Linear(hidden_dim, 2),
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        modality_logits = self.modality_selector(x)
        modality_probs = torch.softmax(modality_logits, dim=1)
        combined = torch.cat([x, modality_probs], dim=1)
        decision_logits = self.decision_predictor(combined)
        return decision_logits, modality_logits


# Calculate Trust Calibration Error
def calculate_trust_calibration_error(
    predicted_decisions, llm_correct, llm_certainty, n_bins=5
):
    """Calculate Trust Calibration Error (lower is better)."""
    predicted_decisions = (
        predicted_decisions.cpu().numpy()
        if torch.is_tensor(predicted_decisions)
        else predicted_decisions
    )
    llm_correct = (
        llm_correct.cpu().numpy() if torch.is_tensor(llm_correct) else llm_correct
    )
    llm_certainty = (
        llm_certainty.cpu().numpy() if torch.is_tensor(llm_certainty) else llm_certainty
    )

    bins = np.linspace(0, 1, n_bins + 1)
    tce = 0.0
    valid_bins = 0

    for i in range(n_bins):
        mask = (llm_certainty >= bins[i]) & (llm_certainty < bins[i + 1])
        if mask.sum() > 0:
            trust_rate = predicted_decisions[mask].mean()
            accuracy_rate = llm_correct[mask].mean()
            tce += abs(trust_rate - accuracy_rate)
            valid_bins += 1

    return tce / max(valid_bins, 1)


# Calculate Appropriate Reliance Rate
def calculate_appropriate_reliance(predicted_decisions, llm_correct):
    """Calculate ARR."""
    predicted_decisions = (
        predicted_decisions.cpu().numpy()
        if torch.is_tensor(predicted_decisions)
        else predicted_decisions
    )
    llm_correct = (
        llm_correct.cpu().numpy() if torch.is_tensor(llm_correct) else llm_correct
    )
    correct_acceptances = np.sum((predicted_decisions == 1) & (llm_correct == 1))
    correct_rejections = np.sum((predicted_decisions == 0) & (llm_correct == 0))
    return (correct_acceptances + correct_rejections) / len(predicted_decisions)


# Calculate Trust Calibration Score
def calculate_trust_calibration(predicted_decisions, llm_correct):
    """Calculate TCS."""
    predicted_decisions = (
        predicted_decisions.cpu().numpy()
        if torch.is_tensor(predicted_decisions)
        else predicted_decisions
    )
    llm_correct = (
        llm_correct.cpu().numpy() if torch.is_tensor(llm_correct) else llm_correct
    )
    trust_rate = np.mean(predicted_decisions)
    accuracy_rate = np.mean(llm_correct)
    return 1.0 - abs(trust_rate - accuracy_rate)


# Training function with optional scheduler
def train_model(
    model,
    train_loader,
    val_loader,
    dataset_name,
    experiment_type,
    num_epochs=50,
    patience=10,
    use_scheduler=False,
):
    """Train with early stopping on ARR+TCS-TCE."""
    criterion_decision = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
    criterion_modality = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
    optimizer = optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=0.0005)

    scheduler = None
    if use_scheduler:
        scheduler = optim.lr_scheduler.ReduceLROnPlateau(
            optimizer, mode="max", factor=0.5, patience=5, verbose=True, min_lr=1e-6
        )

    best_metric = 0.0
    patience_counter = 0
    best_epoch = 0
    best_model_state = model.state_dict()

    for epoch in range(num_epochs):
        # Training phase
        model.train()
        train_loss = 0.0
        train_predictions = []
        train_llm_correct_list = []
        train_llm_certainty_list = []

        for (
            features_batch,
            decisions_batch,
            llm_correct_batch,
            optimal_modality_batch,
        ) in train_loader:
            features_batch = features_batch.to(device)
            decisions_batch = decisions_batch.to(device)
            llm_correct_batch = llm_correct_batch.to(device)
            optimal_modality_batch = optimal_modality_batch.to(device)

            optimizer.zero_grad()
            decision_outputs, modality_outputs = model(features_batch)

            loss_decision = criterion_decision(decision_outputs, decisions_batch)
            loss_modality = criterion_modality(modality_outputs, optimal_modality_batch)
            loss = loss_decision + 0.3 * loss_modality

            loss.backward()
            optimizer.step()

            train_loss += loss.item()
            _, predicted = torch.max(decision_outputs, 1)

            train_predictions.extend(predicted.cpu().numpy())
            train_llm_correct_list.extend(llm_correct_batch.cpu().numpy())
            train_llm_certainty_list.extend(features_batch[:, 0].cpu().numpy())

        train_loss /= len(train_loader)
        train_arr = calculate_appropriate_reliance(
            np.array(train_predictions), np.array(train_llm_correct_list)
        )
        train_tcs = calculate_trust_calibration(
            np.array(train_predictions), np.array(train_llm_correct_list)
        )
        train_tce = calculate_trust_calibration_error(
            np.array(train_predictions),
            np.array(train_llm_correct_list),
            np.array(train_llm_certainty_list),
        )

        # Validation phase
        model.eval()
        val_loss = 0.0
        val_predictions = []
        val_llm_correct_list = []
        val_llm_certainty_list = []

        with torch.no_grad():
            for (
                features_batch,
                decisions_batch,
                llm_correct_batch,
                optimal_modality_batch,
            ) in val_loader:
                features_batch = features_batch.to(device)
                decisions_batch = decisions_batch.to(device)
                llm_correct_batch = llm_correct_batch.to(device)
                optimal_modality_batch = optimal_modality_batch.to(device)

                decision_outputs, modality_outputs = model(features_batch)

                loss_decision = criterion_decision(decision_outputs, decisions_batch)
                loss_modality = criterion_modality(
                    modality_outputs, optimal_modality_batch
                )
                loss = loss_decision + 0.3 * loss_modality

                val_loss += loss.item()
                _, predicted = torch.max(decision_outputs, 1)

                val_predictions.extend(predicted.cpu().numpy())
                val_llm_correct_list.extend(llm_correct_batch.cpu().numpy())
                val_llm_certainty_list.extend(features_batch[:, 0].cpu().numpy())

        val_loss /= len(val_loader)
        val_arr = calculate_appropriate_reliance(
            np.array(val_predictions), np.array(val_llm_correct_list)
        )
        val_tcs = calculate_trust_calibration(
            np.array(val_predictions), np.array(val_llm_correct_list)
        )
        val_tce = calculate_trust_calibration_error(
            np.array(val_predictions),
            np.array(val_llm_correct_list),
            np.array(val_llm_certainty_list),
        )

        # Get current learning rate
        current_lr = optimizer.param_groups[0]["lr"]

        # Store metrics
        experiment_data[experiment_type][dataset_name]["losses"]["train"].append(
            train_loss
        )
        experiment_data[experiment_type][dataset_name]["losses"]["val"].append(val_loss)
        experiment_data[experiment_type][dataset_name]["arr"]["train"].append(train_arr)
        experiment_data[experiment_type][dataset_name]["arr"]["val"].append(val_arr)
        experiment_data[experiment_type][dataset_name]["tcs"]["train"].append(train_tcs)
        experiment_data[experiment_type][dataset_name]["tcs"]["val"].append(val_tcs)
        experiment_data[experiment_type][dataset_name]["tce"]["train"].append(train_tce)
        experiment_data[experiment_type][dataset_name]["tce"]["val"].append(val_tce)
        experiment_data[experiment_type][dataset_name]["epochs"].append(epoch)
        experiment_data[experiment_type][dataset_name]["lr"].append(current_lr)

        # Combined metric for early stopping
        combined_metric = val_arr + val_tcs - val_tce

        # Update scheduler if used
        if scheduler is not None:
            scheduler.step(combined_metric)

        if epoch % 5 == 0 or epoch == num_epochs - 1:
            print(
                f"Epoch {epoch}: train_loss={train_loss:.4f}, val_loss={val_loss:.4f}, LR={current_lr:.6f}"
            )
            print(
                f"  Train - ARR={train_arr:.4f}, TCS={train_tcs:.4f}, TCE={train_tce:.4f}"
            )
            print(f"  Val   - ARR={val_arr:.4f}, TCS={val_tcs:.4f}, TCE={val_tce:.4f}")

        # Early stopping
        if combined_metric > best_metric:
            best_metric = combined_metric
            best_epoch = epoch
            patience_counter = 0
            best_model_state = model.state_dict()
        else:
            patience_counter += 1
            if patience_counter >= patience:
                print(
                    f"\nEarly stopping at epoch {epoch}. Best epoch: {best_epoch} with metric {best_metric:.4f}"
                )
                break

    model.load_state_dict(best_model_state)
    return model, best_epoch

        experiment_data["ablation_with_scheduler"][dataset]["arr"]["val"][-1]
        - experiment_data["baseline_constant_lr"][dataset]["arr"]["val"][-1]
    )
    tcs_improvement = (
        experiment_data["ablation_with_scheduler"][dataset]["tcs"]["val"][-1]
        - experiment_data["baseline_constant_lr"][dataset]["tcs"]["val"][-1]
    )
    tce_improvement = (
        experiment_data["baseline_constant_lr"][dataset]["tce"]["val"][-1]
        - experiment_data["ablation_with_scheduler"][dataset]["tce"]["val"][-1]
    )

    print("\nIMPROVEMENT (Scheduler vs Baseline):")
    print(f"  ARR: {arr_improvement:+.4f}")
    print(f"  TCS: {tcs_improvement:+.4f}")
    print(f"  TCE: {tce_improvement:+.4f} (lower is better)")

print("\n" + "=" * 80)
baseline_avg_arr = np.mean(
    [experiment_data["baseline_constant_lr"][d]["arr"]["val"][-1] for d in datasets]
)
baseline_avg_tcs = np.mean(
    [experiment_data["baseline_constant_lr"][d]["tcs"]["val"][-1] for d in datasets]
)
baseline_avg_tce = np.mean(
    [experiment_data["baseline_constant_lr"][d]["tce"]["val"][-1] for d in datasets]
)

ablation_avg_arr = np.mean(
    [experiment_data["ablation_with_scheduler"][d]["arr"]["val"][-1] for d in datasets]
)
ablation_avg_tcs = np.mean(
    [experiment_data["ablation_with_scheduler"][d]["tcs"]["val"][-1] for d in datasets]
)
ablation_avg_tce = np.mean(
    [experiment_data["ablation_with_scheduler"][d]["tce"]["val"][-1] for d in datasets]
)

print("OVERALL AVERAGES:")
print(
    f"Baseline - ARR: {baseline_avg_arr:.4f}, TCS: {baseline_avg_tcs:.4f}, TCE: {baseline_avg_tce:.4f}"
)
print(
    f"Ablation - ARR: {ablation_avg_arr:.4f}, TCS: {ablation_avg_tcs:.4f}, TCE: {ablation_avg_tce:.4f}"
)
print(
    f"Overall Improvement - ARR: {ablation_avg_arr - baseline_avg_arr:+.4f}, TCS: {ablation_avg_tcs - baseline_avg_tcs:+.4f}, TCE: {baseline_avg_tce - ablation_avg_tce:+.4f}"
)
print("=" * 80)

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")

try:
    experiment_data = np.load(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), allow_pickle=True
    ).item()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading experiment data: {e}")
    exit()

datasets = ["squad", "boolq", "hellaswag"]
colors_baseline = ["#2E86AB", "#A23B72", "#F18F01"]
colors_ablation = ["#006BA6", "#7B2869", "#C77700"]

# Plot 1: ARR comparison across datasets
try:
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
    for i, dataset in enumerate(datasets):
        plt.plot(
            experiment_data["baseline_constant_lr"][dataset]["epochs"],
            experiment_data["baseline_constant_lr"][dataset]["arr"]["val"],
            label=f"{dataset.upper()} Baseline",
            color=colors_baseline[i],
            linestyle="--",
            linewidth=2,
        )
        plt.plot(
            experiment_data["ablation_with_scheduler"][dataset]["epochs"],
            experiment_data["ablation_with_scheduler"][dataset]["arr"]["val"],

            color=colors_ablation[i],
            linewidth=2,
        )
